title: Nursery Rhyme. DLXX. Natural History.
content:
Pretty John Watts,
        We are troubled with rats,
        Will you drive them out of the house?
        We have mice, too, in plenty,
        That feast in the pantry;
        But let them stay,
        And nibble away;
        What harm in a little brown mouse?
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. DLXX. Natural History."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
